examples.title: Project Size Classifier
examples.description: Algorithm to classify project size based on Lines of Code
examples.code:
FUNCTION ClassifyProjectSize(LOC):
 IF LOC < 10000 THEN
 RETURN "Small Project"
 ELSE IF LOC < 100000 THEN
 RETURN "Medium Project"
 ELSE IF LOC < 1000000 THEN
 RETURN "Large Project"
 ELSE
 RETURN "Very Large Project"
 END IF
END FUNCTION

// Example usage
projectSize ← ClassifyProjectSize(500000)
OUTPUT "Project classified as:", projectSize
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates how projects are categorized by size using LOC thresholds defined in software engineering standards.

examples.title: COCOMO Effort Estimator
examples.description: Basic COCOMO model implementation to estimate development effort
examples.code:
FUNCTION CalculateCOCOMO(projectType, KLOC):
 SWITCH projectType:
 CASE "Organic":
 a ← 2.4; b ← 1.05
 CASE "Semi-detached":
 a ← 3.0; b ← 1.12
 CASE "Embedded":
 a ← 3.6; b ← 1.20
 END SWITCH
 
 // Basic COCOMO equation
 effort ← a × (KLOC ^ b)
 developmentTime ← 2.5 × (effort ^ 0.35)
 peopleRequired ← effort / developmentTime
 
 RETURN effort, developmentTime, peopleRequired
END FUNCTION

// Example: 100 KLOC embedded project
effort, time, people ← CalculateCOCOMO("Embedded", 100)
examples.explanation: Implements the basic COCOMO model to estimate effort in person-months, development time, and required personnel based on project type and size.

examples.title: Safety Criticality Level Checker
examples.description: Determines safety criticality level based on application domain
examples.code:
FUNCTION GetSafetyLevel(applicationDomain):
 SWITCH applicationDomain:
 CASE "Medical_devices", "Aerospace_control":
 RETURN "Level A - Highest Criticality"
 CASE "Railway_signaling", "Automotive_brakes":
 RETURN "Level B - High Criticality"
 CASE "Industrial_control", "Telecom_systems":
 RETURN "Level C - Medium Criticality"
 CASE "Entertainment", "Office_applications":
 RETURN "Level D - Low Criticality"
 DEFAULT:
 RETURN "Level D - Low Criticality"
 END SWITCH
END FUNCTION

FUNCTION GetTestingRequirements(safetyLevel):
 IF safetyLevel CONTAINS "Level A" THEN
 RETURN "Formal methods, mathematical verification, 100% MC/DC coverage"
 ELSE IF safetyLevel CONTAINS "Level B" THEN
 RETURN "Rigorous testing, >90% branch coverage"
 ELSE IF safetyLevel CONTAINS "Level C" THEN
 RETURN "Moderate testing, functional coverage"
 ELSE
 RETURN "Standard testing practices"
 END IF
END FUNCTION

// Example usage
level ← GetSafetyLevel("Medical_devices")
requirements ← GetTestingRequirements(level)
examples.explanation: Demonstrates how application domain determines safety criticality levels and corresponding testing requirements in safety-critical software development.
